meaning: adj.证据的,可作证据的,提供证据的
examples: The writer discusses the evaluation of evidential matter in two ways.
Evidential languages force speakers to think hard about how they learned what they say they know.
The evidential requirement for police search should be differential according to the different authority origins.
He said a firm evidential basis needed to be established before any such campaign was introduced.
All of that is well established, but from here the evidential waters get murkier.
